SimulatorPage.heading: TandaOmni Simulator
SimulatorPage.subtitle: Estimate your zero-interest, community-funded savings plan. Built for the Diplomado de Desarrollo y Optimización de Sitios Web 1° - UNAM, by Christopher Guzman.

realEstate.label: Real Estate
realEstate.mitigation: Legal Deed Trust (Reserva de Dominio) required. Your deed is held in trust until the group completes all payments.

vehicle.label: Vehicle / Equipment
vehicle.mitigation: Mandatory platform-held insurance policy required throughout the entire group term - regardless of whether the vehicle is new or used.

travel.label: Travel & Experiences
travel.mitigation: Escrow Lock: Payout releases only after 60% of the current asset cost is funded. A credit card pre-authorization hold will be placed.

SimulatorPage.escrowNote: Payout releases once contributions reach 60% of the current asset cost.
SimulatorPage.emptyState: Enter an asset price above to see your simulation.
SimulatorPage.bankInterestNotice: A traditional loan charges interest on the financed balance for the full term - money paid on top of the asset price.
SimulatorPage.consumerLenderNotice: Mexican consumer lenders advertise a CAT (annual total cost, sin IVA) of 78%–456%. Over a typical short term that means repaying nearly double what you borrowed - and the real cash cost is higher once 16% IVA is added.
SimulatorPage.savingsNote: Total saved versus a traditional bank loan over the same term.

explain.priceLabelGText: The base value of the asset you want to acquire. This is the goal amount your tanda group will collectively fund. All monthly payments, downpayment amounts, and projections are derived from this figure.
explain.termText: The total duration of your savings group in months. Each month, one member receives the full group payout. Longer terms produce smaller monthly payments but increase exposure to price changes over time.
explain.deltaText: The estimated annual rate at which the asset's price changes. Positive for appreciating assets like real estate (+4%–+6%). For vehicles, the direction depends on what you are buying: positive (+3%–+6%) if targeting a brand-new model (tracks MSRP inflation each model year), or negative (−5%–10%) if targeting a specific used vehicle (tracks depreciation while it ages in the queue). This rate is used to calculate your fixed monthly payment and the projected asset price at term end.
explain.downpaymentText: An upfront payment as a percentage of the target price, paid before the group begins. It reduces the monthly burden for all members and secures your position in the payout queue.
explain.monthlyPaymentText: Your fixed monthly contribution. Calculated by averaging the changing asset price across the full term: P = [G × (1 + δ × T/2) − G × d] / M. Every member pays this same amount regardless of when they receive their payout.
explain.downpaymentRequiredText: The actual peso amount of your downpayment (G × d). This must be paid upfront to activate your position in the group's payout queue.
explain.groupSizeText: The number of participants in your tanda group. Group size is independent of the term and does NOT change your monthly payment - in a ROSCA every member funds exactly one asset, so the payment is set by the term alone. Instead, group size controls the payout cadence: a smaller group funds members less often (a larger payout, spaced further apart), while a larger group funds members more frequently.
explain.payoutCadenceText: How often the group funds a member's asset. The group buys N assets over the term (one per member), so payouts happen every M / N months. A smaller group means fewer, more widely spaced payouts; a larger group means more frequent ones. Your own wait depends on your position in the queue.
explain.assetPriceAtEndText: The projected price of the asset at the end of the full term, calculated using the Delta Curve: G₂ = G × (1 + δ)^T. This represents what the last member in the payout queue will pay for their asset.
explain.totalContributedText: The total amount each member pays over the full term: (P × M) + downpayment. Because TandaOmni charges zero interest, this closely matches the original asset price - your community funds each other at no cost.
explain.escrowText: For travel & experiences (unrecoverable assets), your payout is locked until your total contributions reach 60% of the current asset cost. This protects the group against funding an experience that cannot be refunded if a member defaults.
explain.bankAprText: The annual percentage rate a traditional lender charges on the financed balance. Defaults reflect US June 2026 averages (mortgage ~6.5%, new auto ~7%, used auto ~12%, travel/personal loan ~14%). Adjust the slider to match a quote you've received.
explain.catText: Mexico's regulated all-in cost of borrowing. Unlike a nominal APR, the CAT is an effective (compounded) annual rate and folds in interest, commissions, fees, and mandatory insurance. Quoted 'sin IVA', it excludes the 16% VAT on interest, so your real cash cost is a bit higher.
explain.bankFinancedText: The portion of the asset price the bank lends you: the target price minus your downpayment. Interest accrues on this balance.
explain.bankTotalInterestText: The total interest paid to the bank over the full term: (monthly payment × months) − amount financed. This is pure cost on top of the asset - exactly what TandaOmni eliminates.
explain.bankTotalCostText: Everything you pay through the bank: downpayment + (monthly payment × months). Compare this against TandaOmni's Total Contributed to see your savings.
explain.cetesText: The pooled downpayments and monthly contributions sit in the group treasury between payouts. Investing that idle balance in CETES (Mexican government treasury bills, ~6.5% annually) compounds the surplus and strengthens the pool. Move the slider to simulate different yields.

SimulatorPage.combinedChartNote: Cumulative amount paid - {tanda}/mo with TandaOmni vs. {lender}/mo with the lender - plotted against the asset's price as it moves at the {pct}% annual delta.
SimulatorPage.treasuryChartNote: On day one the group pools every member's downpayment ({pool}). Each month it collects {monthly} in contributions and funds one member's asset. Invested at the {rate}% CETES yield, the idle balance compounds - the gap to the grey line is the value of investing.

AnalysisPage.heading: TandaOmni - Product Analysis
AnalysisPage.subtitle: A zero-interest, community-funded financial ecosystem supporting Real Estate, Vehicles, and Experiences. Built for the Diplomado de Desarrollo y Optimización de Sitios Web 1° - UNAM.

overview.objective: A zero-interest, community-funded financial ecosystem (ROSCA/Tanda model) supporting Real Estate (Appreciating), Vehicles/Equipment (Destructible), and Experiences (Unrecoverable). The platform dynamically groups users into specific risk tiers based on the asset class they are funding.
overview.problem: Acquiring high-value assets traditionally forces people into predatory interest-bearing credit, while informal tandas (ROSCAs) lack the financial controls to handle assets whose value shifts over time - real estate that appreciates, vehicles that depreciate, and experiences that are unrecoverable once consumed. TandaOmni replaces interest with a community-funded model that prices each asset class with its own delta curve, mitigates default risk per tier (downpayments, mandatory insurance, escrow locks), and keeps an append-only treasury ledger - making zero-interest collective financing safe, transparent, and viable across appreciating, destructible, and unrecoverable goals.
overview.courseValue: Diplomado de Desarrollo y Optimización de Sitios Web 1° - UNAM

t1.name: Real Estate (Appreciating Asset)
t1.mitigation: 15% Downpayment, Legal Deed Trust (Reserva de Dominio).

t2.name: Vehicles & Commercial Equipment (Depreciating / Destructible)
t2.mitigation: 20% Downpayment, Mandatory Platform-Held Insurance Policy (regardless of delta direction).

t3.name: Travel & Experiences (Unrecoverable)
t3.mitigation: Escrow Lock - Payout only releases after user contributes 60% of total goal. Credit Card Pre-authorization.

vars.N: Number of participants in the group

a.label: A. Dynamic Price Adjustment (Delta Curve)
a.description: Calculates the actual cost to purchase the asset at month m.

b.label: B. Fixed Monthly Payment (Delta Adjusted)
b.description: Averages the asset price over the term using the true compound curve G × (1 + δ)^(m/12), so the fixed payment fully funds the appreciating payouts and the treasury pool breaks even.

c.label: C. Escrow Release Threshold (Tier 3)
c.description: Payout is locked until total paid contributions plus downpayment reach 60% of current asset cost.

d.label: D. Treasury Ledger & Monthly Balance
d.description: Total platform liquidity pool accounting for contributions, yield, payouts, and insurance premiums.

be01.directive: Build User, Group, AssetCategory, and LedgerEntry models. AssetCategory fields: max_term, delta_rate, requires_insurance, escrow_threshold. LedgerEntry MUST be append-only. No updates/deletes.

be02.directive: Build stateless endpoint /api/v1/simulate/. Inputs: G, T, d, category_id. Apply Delta Curve formulas to return N, P and mitigation string.

be03.directive: Build /api/v1/groups/match/. Group KYC-verified users by matching P within 5% variance. Create a Group instance when N target is met. Set status to "AWAITING_DOWNPAYMENT".

be04.directive: Celery task running nightly. Calculate yield for active groups: (balance × (r / 365)). Append YIELD ledger entry.

be05.directive: Celery task running monthly. Check requires_insurance flag. If true, deduct premium and append INSURANCE_FEE ledger entry.

be06.directive: Monthly Celery task. If B_m ≥ G_m, check category escrow rules. If threshold passed, execute PAYOUT ledger entry. Queue order determined by joined date and secure hash.

fe01.directive: Build Simulator.tsx client component. Use tabs for Asset Type (House, Car, Travel). Dynamically adjust slider limits per tier. Debounce calls to /api/v1/simulate/.

fe02.directive: Build dynamic warning rendering. Show mandatory insurance acknowledgment for Tier 2. Show visual 60% Escrow Unlock progress bar for Tier 3.

fe03.directive: Implement NextAuth. Build onboarding wizard capturing ID. Integrate Stripe Elements to capture a pre-authorization hold for Tier 3.

fe04.directive: Fetch user ledger data. Build modular Recharts components: QueueTimeline.tsx showing queue position, and TreasuryChart.tsx tracking group balance (B_m) and yield over time.

do01.directive: Write multi-stage Dockerfiles for Next.js, Django, and Celery workers. Optimize for slim production images.

do02.directive: Create deployment.yaml and service.yaml. Provision two distinct Celery deployments: worker-general and worker-financial-critical. Configure Liveness/Readiness probes.

do03.directive: Configure Ingress controller with routing rules (/api/* to backend, / to frontend). Configure cert-manager for automated Let's Encrypt certificates.

do04.directive: Deploy PostgreSQL StatefulSet with PersistentVolumeClaims (PVCs). Define ConfigMaps and Secrets for environment variables.

do05.directive: Configure a K8s CronJob to trigger pg_dump every 12 hours, piped directly to an encrypted object storage bucket.

infra.intro: TandaOmni runs as a containerized Next.js 16 app (standalone output, App Router, React 19) with a Django/DRF backend, PostgreSQL, and Redis/Celery for async work. The frontend ships as a PWA via Serwist, uses next-intl for multilingual routing, and SimpleWebAuthn for passkey auth. Everything is deployed to a self-hosted Kubernetes cluster sitting behind Cloudflare.
infra.imageAlt: Self-hosted Kubernetes server node running the TandaOmni cluster
infra.imageCaption: Live photo of the self-hosted Kubernetes cluster - 1 master node and 4 worker nodes, all laptops stored in my closet, exposed to the internet through Cloudflare.

specs.orchestration: MicroK8s (Kubernetes), deployed via a versioned Helm chart (apps/tanda/helm).
specs.edge: Cloudflare proxy in front of an nginx ingress controller, terminating public traffic to the cluster.
specs.tls: Automatic HTTPS certificates issued by cert-manager via the letsencrypt-prod cluster issuer.
specs.container: Standalone Next.js Docker image, NODE_ENV=production, telemetry disabled.
specs.resources: Requests 100m CPU / 128Mi memory; limits 500m CPU / 512Mi memory.
specs.probes: HTTP startup probe (5s delay, 30 retries) and liveness probe (10s period) on /.
